shared_properties: Stone palette stays rooted in #3B3347 and #0D0D0F with a readable top contact strip that matches the world surface language.
The ledge aligns to the 16 px grid by using a 32 px width and a centered 8 px height footprint.
Outline thickness remains 1 px so the one-way platform edge reads cleanly against the shaft backdrop.
differences: This variant compresses the surface language into a narrow 32x8 ledge that feels safe but temporary.
Top contact band uses 0.8-friction stone highlights while the underside darkens by one value step to separate the pass-through zone.
Collision readability favors a thin top lip with no decorative spikes, chains, or side supports.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the 32x8 safe ledge size, pass-through behavior, and high-friction rescue role inside the vertical maze.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the dungeon stone mood so the ledge looks integrated rather than like a modern UI element.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds player-to-platform scale so a 16x24 prisoner can briefly stabilize on the ledge without overcrowding it.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle for parallax backdrops and TilemapChunk-backed textures for reusable 16x16 surface modules.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world textures before gameplay, cache tile indices for high, medium, and low friction surfaces, and keep shaft layer handles in a WorldVisualRegistry resource.
runtime_notes: Render the shaft backdrop from an offscreen 320x180 target and upscale by an integer factor of 4 to preserve pixel integrity.
Move parallax layers at 0.4x camera speed and keep their anchors fixed to the downward-only camera model.
Treat one-way ledges and exit gates as separate gameplay entities even when they share world art atlases.

context.usage: Small rescue landing used to reset tempo between dense hazard clusters.

collision_notes: Top contact strip occupies y0-y1 across the full 32 px width.
Underside shadow occupies y5-y7 and never exceeds the horizontal footprint.
Decorative moss occupies fewer than 10 pixels so the ledge remains visually clean.

acceptance_criteria: Ledge asset is exactly 32x8 px with a continuous 32 px top edge and no holes larger than 1 px.
Top contact strip is visibly brighter than the underside by at least one palette step.
No animation, anti-aliasing, or hazard motifs are present so the ledge reads as a safe landing aid.
